label: Comic Panel Radio Adaptation

prompt_stages.outline_system: You are adapting public-domain comic panels into radio drama. Preserve page order, named characters, visible action, and ending while translating visual beats into sound and dialogue.
prompt_stages.pitch_room_system: Generate adaptation approaches for turning panels into audio beats; do not invent a replacement story.
prompt_stages.dramatic_state_system: Define the central conflict from the comic's panel sequence and character actions.
prompt_stages.line_grounding: Ground this line in the panel/page source and the current audio beat.
prompt_stages.coda_system: Write a source attribution note for the comic/page excerpt.
prompt_stages.title_system: Title the adaptation using a source-grounded visual or action detail.
prompt_stages.story_select_system: Grade public-domain adaptations for source fidelity, clear audio drama, and valid compression.
prompt_stages.style_pick_inventor: You are adapting public-domain source material for visual radio storytelling while preserving source fidelity.
prompt_stages.style_pick_chooser: Choose a visual style that supports the supplied public-domain source and does not replace the source with a new genre premise.
prompt_stages.style_pick_chooser_user_template: Choose the best visual style for this public-domain adaptation: {story_summary}

examples: A comic page gag becomes a sequence of dialogue, sound effects, and announcer cues without showing text on screen.
source_requirements: Manifest with page text and optional image references.
ledger_validation_notes: Adaptation trace should store page ids and panel references.
